---
title: "Case 5: $\\chi_{c0,2}\\to K\\bar K\\pi\\pi$"
---

**Status:** <span class="badge-open">open</span> — the four-body frontier;
depends on cases 1–3 and on the kaon C-convention note.

## Setting

Four-body strong decay of a C- and P-eigenstate:
$\chi_{c0},\chi_{c2}$ with $I^G J^{PC} = 0^+0^{++},\,0^+2^{++}$. Final state
$K^+(1)\,K^-(2)\,\pi^+(3)\,\pi^-(4)$ (and coupled neutral modes) is globally
self-conjugate. Constraints to enforce: $I_{\text{tot}}=0$,
$C_{\text{tot}}=+$.

Topologies:

- $(K\bar K)(\pi\pi)$ — quasi-two-body, both pairs self-conjugate;
- cascade chains with excited kaons: $K_1\bar K$, $K^*\bar K^*$,
  $K^*(\to K\pi)\,\bar K\pi$, etc., where the C bookkeeping requires the
  current-level $\eta_C$ signs;
- the $K_1(1270)/K_1(1400)$ complication: physical states mix
  $K_{1A}$ ($\eta_C=+1$-type) and $K_{1B}$ ($\eta_C=-1$-type), so C
  constraints must be imposed in the $(K_{1A},K_{1B})$ basis and rotated —
  see `docs/isospin-kaon-conventions.qmd`.

## The central conceptual question (resolved in principle)

Positive C parity suggests symmetrizing over the simultaneous swap
$K^+\leftrightarrow K^-$, $\pi^+\leftrightarrow\pi^-$. The worry was: under
this swap the system "does not go into itself", so what to do with the
$\chi_{c2}$ spin — is $A(p_1,p_2,p_3,p_4)+A(p_2,p_1,p_4,p_3)$ even correct?

The resolution proposed in the [C chapter](../../03-charge-conjugation.qmd):
C is internal, so at the level of the **fixed-frame** amplitude the parent
polarization is untouched and the constraint *is*
$$
M_{\lambda_0}(p_1,p_2,p_3,p_4)
= \eta_C^{(\chi)}\,\kappa\;
M_{\lambda_0}(p_2,p_1,p_4,p_3),
\qquad \eta_C^{(\chi)}=+1,
$$
with $\kappa$ a constant collecting the meson C phases. All $\chi_{c2}$-spin
complications live in the translation to chain-aligned amplitudes, where the
momentum permutation drags Wigner rotations onto $\lambda_0$; for four-body
kinematics the configuration is no longer planar, so the compensating
rotation $r_\pi$ is a full rotation, not just a sign — this is the genuinely
new element compared to the three-body cases. **Claim to be verified**
(ledger L5).

## To do

1. Pin $\kappa$ for $K^+K^-\pi^+\pi^-$ from the current conventions
   ($C|K^\pm\rangle$, $C|\pi^\pm\rangle$ phases; expected $\kappa=+1$ for
   pseudoscalars, derive rather than assume).
2. Scalar parent first: for $\chi_{c0}$, $\lambda_0=0$ and no alignment
   subtlety on the parent — derive chain-level relations for the
   $(K\bar K^{(*)})$-type chains, including the $\eta_X$ signs
   ($K^*$: $-$, $K_{1A}$: $+$, $K_{1B}$: $-$, $K_2^*$: from its current).
3. $\chi_{c2}$: compute the alignment rotation induced by the momentum
   permutation in the chosen four-body reference orientation; verify
   numerically (this is the first non-planar $r_\pi$).
4. Individual decay chains: understand the sign between channels chain by
   chain ($K_1\bar K$ vs $K\bar K_1$ etc.) in the $(K_{1A},K_{1B})$ basis,
   then rotate to physical $K_1$'s.
5. Isospin: couple to $I_{\text{tot}}=0$ with the doublet conventions of the
   kaon note; keep isospin Clebsch signs separated from C signs.
6. Cross-check: independent evaluation via explicit symmetrization
   (route (a)) vs coupling relations (route (b)); tensor bridge for one or
   two representative chains.

## Dependencies

- Case 1 (spinless swaps), Case 3 (C on fermionless… rather, C machinery and
  parity phases), and the kaon current-convention note are prerequisites.
- Four-body alignment conventions in `CascadeDecays.jl` must be documented in
  the registry before step 3.
